page: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dalsv7 family. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. The system features hyper-threading with 2 threads per core, 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression single-threaded tests all rank in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 and PassMark CPU physics and encryption tests score in the top 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average category. Small model LLM inference shows strong performance, while larger models process at average speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32als_v7 offers high compute density per dollar due to its high-frequency AMD processor, but presents a clear tradeoff in memory capacity. With only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and no local storage, it is less suitable for memory-intensive databases or massive data-caching layers. Instead, the hardware profile and benchmark results suggest a strong fit for compute-bound workloads that benefit from high single-thread performance, such as web serving, file compression utilities, lightweight machine learning inference, and general-purpose compute tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, it features a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and lacks local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core, gzip compression, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. The instance offers high compute density but presents a tradeoff in memory capacity. It is best suited for compute-bound workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight LLM inference, and web serving applications that do not require large memory allocations or local disk storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 is a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC server running at 4.5 GHz with 64 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for compute-bound workloads but has limited memory per core.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v7 features 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense virtual server featuring top-tier single-core performance and a streamlined memory-to-CPU ratio for dedicated processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU, compression, and decompression benchmark performance
No local storage, requiring external premium storage volumes
Suited for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference
